paper_title: Dirac particles in the field of Frost-Musulin diatomic potential and the thermodynamic properties via parametric Nikiforov-Uvarov method

methods.description: This method is used to solve the Dirac equation for particles in the field of the Frost-Musulin diatomic potential. It involves using the parametric Nikiforov-Uvarov method to obtain energy eigenvalues and eigenfunctions, which are essential for understanding the quantum behavior of the system.

steps.description: Apply the parametric Nikiforov-Uvarov method to the Dirac equation with the Frost-Musulin potential.
steps.searchable_summary: solve Dirac equation using Nikiforov-Uvarov method for Frost-Musulin potential

steps.description: Calculate the energy eigenvalues and eigenfunctions from the solved equation.

methods.description: This method calculates the thermodynamic properties of the system using the obtained energy eigenvalues. These properties include partition functions, specific heat, and entropy, which are crucial for understanding the thermodynamic behavior of the system.

steps.description: Use the energy eigenvalues to calculate the partition function of the system.
steps.searchable_summary: compute partition function from energy eigenvalues

steps.description: Calculate the specific heat from the partition function.
steps.searchable_summary: derive specific heat from partition function

steps.description: Determine the entropy of the system using the partition function.
